generations.code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    ops = []
    
    def rev(p):
        ops.append(p)
        a[:p] = a[:p][::-1]
    
    def bring_to_front(pos, limit):
        # pos is 0-indexed, bring a[pos] to a[0] using odd-length reversals within limit
        p = pos + 1  # 1-indexed
        if p % 2 == 1:
            if p > 1:
                rev(p)
        else:
            # p is even
            rev(p - 1)  # p-1 is odd
            # now element is still at position p (0-indexed: pos)
            # reverse p+1 (odd)
            rev(p + 1)
            # element was at pos p (1-indexed), after rev(p-1): still at p
            # after rev(p+1): element at p goes to position (p+1)-p+1-1 = 1 (0-indexed)? 
            # rev(p+1) reverses indices 0..p, element at index p-1 (0-indexed) goes to index 0
            # Wait let me recheck
    
    m = n
    while m >= 3:
        big = m
        small = m - 1
        
        pos_big = a.index(big)
        bring_to_front(pos_big, m)
        rev(m)
        # big is now at position m-1 (0-indexed)
        
        pos_small = a.index(small)
        bring_to_front(pos_small, m - 1)
        if (m - 1) % 2 == 1:
            rev(m - 1)
        else:
            # m-1 is even, can't do direct reversal
            # This is a problem
            pass
        
        m -= 2
    
    # Check if sorted
    if a == list(range(1, n+1)):
        print(len(ops))
        print(*ops)
    else:
        print(-1)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    ops = []
    
    def rev(p):
        ops.append(p)
        a[:p] = a[:p][::-1]
    
    def bring_to_front(pos, limit):
        # pos is 0-indexed, bring a[pos] to a[0] using odd-length reversals within limit
        p = pos + 1  # 1-indexed
        if p % 2 == 1:
            if p > 1:
                rev(p)
        else:
            # p is even
            rev(p - 1)  # p-1 is odd
            # now element is still at position p (0-indexed: pos)
            # reverse p+1 (odd)
            rev(p + 1)
            # element was at pos p (1-indexed), after rev(p-1): still at p
            # after rev(p+1): element at p goes to position (p+1)-p+1-1 = 1 (0-indexed)? 
            # rev(p+1) reverses indices 0..p, element at index p-1 (0-indexed) goes to index 0
            # Wait let me recheck
    
    m = n
    while m >= 3:
        big = m
        small = m - 1
        
        pos_big = a.index(big)
        bring_to_front(pos_big, m)
        rev(m)
        # big is now at position m-1 (0-indexed)
        
        pos_small = a.index(small)
        bring_to_front(pos_small, m - 1)
        if (m - 1) % 2 == 1:
            rev(m - 1)
        else:
            # m-1 is even, can't do direct reversal
            # This is a problem
            pass
        
        m -= 2
    
    # Check if sorted
    if a == list(range(1, n+1)):
        print(len(ops))
        print(*ops)
    else:
        print(-1)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
